title.en: The TV White Space Coexistence System: Architecture and Services
title.zh: 电视白空间共存系统：架构与服务

blocks.en: Imagine several unlicensed wireless networks — built by different vendors, running different radio technologies — all trying to use the same slice of free spectrum, such as the gaps between TV broadcast channels (called television white spaces, or TVWS). None of them owns the band, so none is entitled to be protected from the others. Left alone, they would step on each other's signals. IEEE Std 802.19.1-2018 solves this with a radio-technology-independent Coexistence System: a network-based referee that helps dissimilar or independently operated unlicensed networks share a band without harmful interference.
blocks.zh: 设想有好几个免授权无线网络——由不同厂商建造、运行着不同的无线技术——都想使用同一段空闲频谱，比如电视广播频道之间的空隙（称为电视白空间，TVWS）。它们谁都不拥有这段频段，因此谁也无权要求别人不来干扰自己。如果放任不管，它们的信号就会互相踩踏。IEEE Std 802.19.1-2018 用一个与无线技术无关的共存系统（Coexistence System）来解决这一问题：它是一个基于网络的“裁判”，帮助这些互不相同或各自独立运营的免授权网络共享同一频段而不产生有害干扰。

blocks.en: The standard's Scope (1.1) is exactly that: radio-technology-independent methods for network-based coexistence among dissimilar or independently operated unlicensed devices, defined for geolocation-capable devices operating under general authorization — TVWS, the 5 GHz license-exempt bands, and the 3.5 GHz general authorized access band. Its Purpose (1.2) is to let the whole family of IEEE 802 wireless standards use these bands most effectively, while remaining useful to non-IEEE-802 networks too. 'General authorization' means you may use the spectrum without individual frequency planning — and, crucially, with no right to interference protection from others. That is precisely why a coexistence referee is needed.
blocks.zh: 标准的范围（1.1）正是如此：为运行在通用授权（general authorization）下、具备地理定位能力的设备，定义与无线技术无关、基于网络的共存方法，覆盖 TVWS、5 GHz 免授权频段以及 3.5 GHz 通用授权接入频段。其目的（1.2）是让整个 IEEE 802 无线标准家族最有效地使用这些频段，同时也对非 IEEE 802 网络有用。“通用授权”意味着你可以使用频谱而无需做单独的频率规划——而且关键在于，你无权要求免受他人干扰。这正是为什么需要一个共存“裁判”。

blocks.en: Before the architecture, the standard pins down its vocabulary (definitions in 3.1, acronyms in 3.2). A WSO (white space object) is an entity representing a TVWS device or network of TVWS devices; it connects to a CE to consume coexistence services. A GCO (geolocation-capable object) represents a device or network operating under general authorization with geolocation capability. A CE (coexistence enabler) represents a WSO/GCO inside the system. A CM (coexistence manager) makes the coexistence decisions about reconfiguring WSOs/GCOs. A CDIS (coexistence discovery and information server) figures out, for each CM, which other WSOs/GCOs might affect the ones it serves. A COE (coordination enabler) lets one coexistence system talk to another. 'Coexistence' itself is defined as two or more spectrum-dependent networks operating without harmful interference under the same authorization in the same band.
blocks.zh: 在讲架构之前，标准先确定其术语（定义见 3.1，缩略语见 3.2）。WSO（white space object，白空间对象）是代表某个 TVWS 设备或 TVWS 设备网络的实体，它连接到一个 CE 以消费共存服务。GCO（geolocation-capable object，具备地理定位能力的对象）代表在通用授权下运营、且具备地理定位能力的设备或网络。CE（coexistence enabler，共存使能器）在系统内代表一个 WSO/GCO。CM（coexistence manager，共存管理器）负责做出关于重配置 WSO/GCO 的共存决策。CDIS（coexistence discovery and information server，共存发现与信息服务器）为每个 CM 找出哪些其他 WSO/GCO 可能影响它所服务的对象。COE（coordination enabler，协调使能器）让一个共存系统能与另一个共存系统对话。“共存”本身定义为：两个或更多依赖频谱的网络，在同一授权、同一频段下运营而不产生有害干扰。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 460 250" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="15" y="15" width="200" height="220" rx="6" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="30" y="32" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11">Coexistence System 1</text><rect x="30" y="45" width="75" height="30" rx="4"/><text x="55" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11">CDIS</text><rect x="125" y="45" width="75" height="30" rx="4"/><text x="152" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11">CM</text><rect x="125" y="110" width="75" height="30" rx="4"/><text x="154" y="129" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11">CE</text><rect x="125" y="175" width="75" height="40" rx="4"/><text x="135" y="192" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10">WSO / GCO</text><rect x="30" y="110" width="75" height="30" rx="4"/><text x="50" y="129" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11">COE</text><line x1="105" y1="60" x2="125" y2="60"/><text x="100" y="55" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9">B2</text><line x1="162" y1="75" x2="162" y2="110"/><text x="166" y="95" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9">B1</text><line x1="162" y1="140" x2="162" y2="175"/><text x="166" y="160" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9">A</text><line x1="67" y1="125" x2="125" y2="125"/><text x="82" y="120" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9">B4</text><rect x="315" y="95" width="130" height="60" rx="6" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="330" y="112" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10">Coexistence Sys 2</text><rect x="345" y="118" width="70" height="28" rx="4"/><text x="368" y="136" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11">COE</text><line x1="67" y1="125" x2="345" y2="132" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/><text x="235" y="120" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9">B5</text></svg>

caption.en: System architecture (4.1): four logical entities (CDIS, CM, CE, COE) joined by logical interfaces. Interface A links a CE to its WSO/GCO; B1 links CE to CM; B2 links CM to CDIS; B3 links two CMs; B4 links a CM to a COE; B5 links two COEs across independent systems. (Interface C, CM to a spectrum management database, is implementation-dependent and not specified.)
caption.zh: 系统架构（4.1）：四个逻辑实体（CDIS、CM、CE、COE）由逻辑接口连接。接口 A 连接 CE 与其 WSO/GCO；B1 连接 CE 与 CM；B2 连接 CM 与 CDIS；B3 连接两个 CM；B4 连接 CM 与 COE；B5 跨独立系统连接两个 COE。（接口 C 连接 CM 与频谱管理数据库，依实现而定，标准未作规定。）

blocks.en: That is the system architecture (4.1): four logical entities and seven logical interfaces (A, B1–B5, C). These are functional roles, not a mandated physical layout — the standard is careful to say the architecture is not any specific implementation. The CDIS provides the coexistence discovery service to the CMs it serves, telling each CM about the potential neighbors of its WSOs/GCOs; it also helps CMs discover each other. The CM provides information service or management service to its WSOs/GCOs (always through their CEs). Interface C — between a CM and a spectrum management database such as a TVWS database — is optional and left to the implementer (one example is the PAWS protocol for TVWS).
blocks.zh: 这就是系统架构（4.1）：四个逻辑实体和七个逻辑接口（A、B1–B5、C）。它们是功能角色，而非强制的物理布局——标准特意声明该架构并不代表任何具体实现。CDIS 向它服务的 CM 提供共存发现服务，告诉每个 CM 它的 WSO/GCO 有哪些潜在邻居；它还帮助各个 CM 互相发现。CM 向它的 WSO/GCO 提供信息服务（information service）或管理服务（management service）——始终通过它们的 CE 进行。接口 C——位于 CM 与某个频谱管理数据库（例如 TVWS 数据库）之间——是可选的，留给实现者决定（一个例子是用于 TVWS 的 PAWS 协议）。

blocks.en: The reference model (4.2) defines how these entities actually communicate, via two service access points (SAPs). The COEX_TR_SAP (transport SAP) lets a CE, CM, COE, and CDIS talk to one another over underlying transport — for example, interface B1 between a CE and a CM rides over TCP/IP. The COEX_MEDIA_SAP (media SAP) defines interface A between a CE and its WSO/GCO. Inside a real device, the CE reaches the radio through management SAPs for the PHY, MAC, and convergence sublayers, with a coexistence primitive mapping (CXPM) function translating generic coexistence primitives into the device's own control commands. A CE has both a COEX_TR_SAP and a COEX_MEDIA_SAP; a CM, COE, or CDIS has only the COEX_TR_SAP.
blocks.zh: 参考模型（4.2）定义了这些实体究竟如何通信，依靠两个服务接入点（SAP）。COEX_TR_SAP（传输 SAP）让 CE、CM、COE 和 CDIS 通过底层传输互相通信——例如 CE 与 CM 之间的接口 B1 就承载在 TCP/IP 之上。COEX_MEDIA_SAP（媒体 SAP）定义 CE 与其 WSO/GCO 之间的接口 A。在真实设备内部，CE 通过 PHY、MAC 和汇聚子层的管理 SAP 触达无线电，并由一个共存原语映射（CXPM）功能把通用的共存原语翻译成设备自身的控制命令。CE 同时具备 COEX_TR_SAP 和 COEX_MEDIA_SAP；而 CM、COE 或 CDIS 只具备 COEX_TR_SAP。

blocks.en: System profiles and interoperability (4.3) keep the system open. The standard defines a common set of data types, primitives, messages, and procedures; each profile uses only a specific subset (detailed in Clause 6). Profiles are designed so that the required interoperability is guaranteed across entities running different profiles. The binding rules are simple: any CDIS must serve any type of CM; any CM must be able to exchange information with any other CM; every CM, COE, and CE must support at least one profile; every CM must support both management and information service; and every CE must support at least one of management or information service (and may support both).
blocks.zh: 系统配置文件与互操作性（4.3）让系统保持开放。标准定义了一套通用的数据类型、原语、消息和过程；每个配置文件（profile）只使用其中特定的一个子集（细节见第 6 章）。配置文件的设计确保了运行不同配置文件的实体之间能达到所需的互操作性。约束规则很简单：任何 CDIS 都必须能服务任何类型的 CM；任何 CM 都必须能与任何其他 CM 交换信息；每个 CM、COE 和 CE 都必须至少支持一个配置文件；每个 CM 都必须同时支持管理服务和信息服务；每个 CE 都必须至少支持管理服务或信息服务之一（也可两者都支持）。

blocks.en:
flowchart TD
  CDIS[CDIS] -->|B2: discovery service| CM[CM]
  CM -->|info service: reports| CE[CE]
  CM -->|mgmt service: reconfig requests| CE
  CE -->|A| WSO[WSO / GCO]
  WSO -->|subscribe + register| CE
blocks.zh:
flowchart TD
  CDIS[CDIS] -->|B2：发现服务| CM[CM]
  CM -->|信息服务：报告| CE[CE]
  CM -->|管理服务：重配请求| CE
  CE -->|A| WSO[WSO / GCO]
  WSO -->|订阅 + 注册| CE

blocks.en: Now the services themselves (4.4). The introduction (4.4.1) splits coexistence services into two categories: services to WSOs/GCOs, and services to CMs. Services for WSOs/GCOs (4.4.2) flow over interface A. To use them, a WSO/GCO must first subscribe and register; afterward it may use information service and/or management service (a WSO may subscribe to only one service at a time). Under information service, the WSO/GCO receives a coexistence report rich enough to make its own autonomous decisions. Under management service, the coexistence system sends it reconfiguration requests, and the WSO/GCO must supply information and perform measurements on request so the system can decide well. Services for the CM (4.4.3) flow over interface B2: a CM, once subscribed and registered to a CDIS, gets the coexistence discovery service — which can be inter-CM only (the CM does its own intra-CM discovery) or full discovery (the CDIS handles both inter-CM and intra-CM).
blocks.zh: 接下来是服务本身（4.4）。引言（4.4.1）把共存服务分为两类：面向 WSO/GCO 的服务，以及面向 CM 的服务。面向 WSO/GCO 的服务（4.4.2）经接口 A 流转。要使用这些服务，WSO/GCO 必须先订阅并注册；之后它可使用信息服务和/或管理服务（一个 WSO 同一时刻只能订阅一种服务）。在信息服务下，WSO/GCO 收到一份足够丰富的共存报告，从而能自主做出决策。在管理服务下，共存系统向它发送重配置请求，而 WSO/GCO 必须按要求提供信息并执行测量，以便系统做出良好决策。面向 CM 的服务（4.4.3）经接口 B2 流转：CM 一旦向某个 CDIS 订阅并注册，就能获得共存发现服务——既可以是仅限 CM 间（inter-CM，此时 CM 自己完成 CM 内 intra-CM 发现），也可以是完整发现（由 CDIS 同时处理 CM 间与 CM 内发现）。

blocks.en: Two services, two mindsets. Information service is advisory — the coexistence system hands the network a report and the network decides for itself. Management service is directive — the coexistence system tells the network how to reconfigure, and the network must feed back information and measurements. Subscribing and registering is the entry ticket to either one.
blocks.zh: 两种服务，两种思路。信息服务是“建议性的”——共存系统把报告交给网络，由网络自己决定。管理服务是“指令性的”——共存系统告诉网络如何重配，网络则必须反馈信息和测量结果。无论选哪一种，订阅并注册都是进入服务的入场券。

blocks.en: Finally, coexistence algorithms (4.5) are the procedures the system runs to deliver those services. The introduction (4.5.1) names two main classes plus a catch-all. Coexistence discovery algorithms (4.5.2) are used by the CDIS and CM to find which WSOs/GCOs may affect each other — for instance, by statistical analysis of expected interference (estimating the interference one WSO causes another and comparing it to a threshold), by coverage-and-interference analysis (a normalized geographic distance versus a technology-dependent threshold), or by methods built for GCOs under geolocation uncertainty, including multi-band operation. Coexistence decision algorithms (4.5.3) are used by a CM to decide how to reconfigure WSOs/GCOs — the standard lists more than twenty, from operating-channel selection (classifying TV channels as allowed, available, protected, restricted, operating, and so on) to negotiation among CMs, fairness-constrained resource allocation, load balancing, and output power control. And other coexistence-related algorithms (4.5.4) cover the rest: balanced sharing (using a coexistence value, or CV, to stop one WSO from grabbing or hoarding resources others need) and temporarily released resource (a mechanism to release, let others use, and later reclaim a portion of allocated resources). The standard stresses that implementations are not limited to any one algorithm.
blocks.zh: 最后，共存算法（4.5）是系统为交付上述服务而运行的过程。引言（4.5.1）点出两大类外加一个兜底类。共存发现算法（4.5.2）由 CDIS 和 CM 用来找出哪些 WSO/GCO 可能互相影响——例如通过对预期干扰的统计分析（估计一个 WSO 对另一个造成的干扰并与阈值比较）、通过覆盖与干扰分析（用归一化的地理距离对比一个与技术相关的阈值），或通过为地理定位存在不确定性的 GCO（包括多频段运行）设计的方法。共存决策算法（4.5.3）由 CM 用来决定如何重配 WSO/GCO——标准列出了二十多种，从工作信道选择（把电视信道分类为 allowed、available、protected、restricted、operating 等）到 CM 间协商、带公平性约束的资源分配、负载均衡，以及发射功率控制。而其他与共存相关的算法（4.5.4）涵盖余下内容：均衡共享（使用共存值 CV，阻止某个 WSO 抢占或囤积他人所需的资源）以及临时释放资源（一种先释放、让他人使用、之后再回收一部分已分配资源的机制）。标准强调，实现并不局限于任何单一算法。

summary.en: Coverage map — where each clause lives
summary.zh: 覆盖地图——每个条款所在之处

blocks.en: This lesson teaches all 14 units of IEEE Std 802.19.1-2018 Clauses 1, 3, and 4. Clause 1 — Overview: 1.1 Scope (radio-technology-independent network-based coexistence for geolocation-capable unlicensed devices under general authorization: TVWS, 5 GHz, 3.5 GHz) and 1.2 Purpose (let the IEEE 802 family use these bands effectively; useful to non-802 networks too). Clause 3 — Definitions, acronyms, and abbreviations: 3.1 Definitions (coexistence, coexistence system, WSO, GCO, CE, CM, CDIS, COE, coexistence set, profile, and the three decision-making topologies) and 3.2 Acronyms and abbreviations (CDIS, CE, CM, COE, GCO, WSO, CV, SMDB, TVWS, and more). Clause 4 — System description: 4.1 System architecture (four entities, seven interfaces A/B1–B5/C, Figure 1); 4.2 Reference model (COEX_TR_SAP, COEX_MEDIA_SAP, CXPM, Figures 2–5); 4.3 System profiles and interoperability (common data set, per-profile subset, the CDIS/CM/COE/CE interoperability rules); 4.4 Coexistence services — 4.4.1 Introduction (two categories), 4.4.2 Services for WSOs/GCOs (subscribe/register over interface A, information vs management service), 4.4.3 Services for CM (discovery service over B2, inter-CM vs full discovery); 4.5 Coexistence algorithms — 4.5.1 Introduction (discovery vs decision classes), 4.5.2 Coexistence discovery algorithms (statistical interference, coverage/interference, geolocation-uncertainty, multi-band), 4.5.3 Coexistence decision algorithms (operating-channel selection and 20+ others), 4.5.4 Other algorithms related to coexistence (balanced sharing via CV, temporarily released resource).
blocks.zh: 本课讲授 IEEE Std 802.19.1-2018 第 1、3、4 章的全部 14 个单元。第 1 章——概述：1.1 范围（为通用授权下具备地理定位能力的免授权设备提供与无线技术无关、基于网络的共存方法：TVWS、5 GHz、3.5 GHz）与 1.2 目的（让 IEEE 802 家族有效使用这些频段，并对非 802 网络也有用）。第 3 章——定义、首字母缩略词与缩写：3.1 定义（共存、共存系统、WSO、GCO、CE、CM、CDIS、COE、共存集、配置文件，以及三种决策拓扑）与 3.2 首字母缩略词与缩写（CDIS、CE、CM、COE、GCO、WSO、CV、SMDB、TVWS 等）。第 4 章——系统描述：4.1 系统架构（四个实体，七个接口 A/B1–B5/C，图 1）；4.2 参考模型（COEX_TR_SAP、COEX_MEDIA_SAP、CXPM，图 2–5）；4.3 系统配置文件与互操作性（通用数据集、各配置文件子集、CDIS/CM/COE/CE 互操作规则）；4.4 共存服务——4.4.1 引言（两类）、4.4.2 面向 WSO/GCO 的服务（经接口 A 订阅/注册，信息服务与管理服务）、4.4.3 面向 CM 的服务（经 B2 的发现服务，CM 间发现与完整发现）；4.5 共存算法——4.5.1 引言（发现类与决策类）、4.5.2 共存发现算法（统计干扰、覆盖/干扰、地理定位不确定性、多频段）、4.5.3 共存决策算法（工作信道选择及 20 余种）、4.5.4 其他与共存相关的算法（基于 CV 的均衡共享、临时释放资源）。

cites.note: 1.1 Scope, 1.2 Purpose, 3.1 Definitions, 3.2 Acronyms/abbreviations, start of 4.1 System architecture (Figure 1).
4.1 entity roles and interfaces; 4.2 Reference model (COEX_TR_SAP, COEX_MEDIA_SAP, CXPM, Figures 2-5); 4.3 System profiles and interoperability.
4.4.1 Introduction, 4.4.2 Services for WSOs/GCOs, 4.4.3 Services for CM; 4.5.1 Introduction, 4.5.2 discovery algorithms, start of 4.5.3.
4.5.3.1 operating-channel selection (TV channel sets); 4.5.4 Other algorithms (4.5.4.1 balanced sharing via CV, 4.5.4.2 temporarily released resource).
